methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $5.0B - $7.6B.

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Joby Aviation, a publicly traded autonomous aircraft manufacturer in the Drones & Autonomous sector, is evaluated against a peer set of three same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers: Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), Nuro (Series G, $6.0B), and Archer Aviation (Public, $5.0B). The broader comparable universe includes six private frontier-tech firms in autonomous systems, though only the three above-named peers provided sufficient data for quantitative triangulation.

Peer-median valuation analysis, the primary applicable method given limited data, yields a $6.0B implied value based on the median of the three comparables, with a range spanning $5.0B to $7.6B. This approach reflects relative positioning within the autonomous vehicle and drone segment, where Joby's public status and capital intensity align with Archer Aviation while maintaining comparability to the later-stage private raises of Zipline and Nuro.

The triangulated valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B (median $6.0B) is materially below Joby's stated $10B+ current valuation. Key uncertainties include the limited peer sample (n=3), reliance on a single methodological pillar, and the absence of revenue or unit economics in the comparative set. The gap between triangulated value and market valuation warrants investigation into near-term commercialization milestones, regulatory pathways, or capital efficiency metrics not captured in peer multiples.
